mnemonics: Recurrence Relation Recipe: Find Base Case (Bake), Assume (Assume), Prove (Prove) for BAP!
Master Theorem Menu: Compare n^log_b(a) to f(n) - is the work done at the Leaves, Root, or Even?
keyPoints: A recurrence relation defines a function in terms of its value on smaller inputs.
The substitution method requires guessing a solution and proving it via mathematical induction.
The Master Theorem provides a cookbook solution for recurrences of the form T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to establish or incorrectly defining the base case for the recurrence.
Making an incorrect guess in the substitution method, leading to an unsuccessful proof.
Misapplying the Master Theorem case (e.g., Case 2 vs Case 2b) or applying it to a recurrence that doesn't fit its standard form.
